summary: Iteration 252 — verified the REVERT of iteration-251 classifier change (transaction_type FIRST, debit_or_credit SECOND with statement convention, amount-sign last) and the new admin-only GET /api/accounting/transactions/{item_id}/diagnostic endpoint. 21 new tests pass + all 45 prior regression tests (iter 248/249/250/251) still pass = 66/66 PASS.

unit_tests_direction_of_reverted: customer_payment + debit_or_credit='debit' -> 'credit' (THE PROD-REPORTED BUG, now fixed by revert)
diagnostic_endpoint_tests: GET /accounting/transactions/{id}/diagnostic by id -> 200 with expected shape (txn_code, stored_direction='debit', classified_now='credit', diagnosis.{zoho_transaction_type, zoho_debit_or_credit, raw_amount_sign='positive'}, raw object)
GET /accounting/transactions/{txn_code}/diagnostic by txn_code -> same expected shape
regression_tests: POST /accounting/transactions/sync responds in <8s (400 fast on preview without Zoho creds)
All 14 iteration_249 tests (zoho scope error translation) pass
All 12 iteration_250 tests (zoho banking scope) pass

All 13 iteration_251 tests pass after updating the 2 cases impacted by the revert (vendor_payment+credit→debit; customer_payment+debit→credit; and seed in reclassify test updated to use customer_payment+stored=debit so the flip still occurs under the reverted priority)

critical_code_review_comments: _direction_of (routes/accounting_transactions.py:92-116) now correctly prioritises transaction_type (curated allowlists _CREDIT_TYPES/_DEBIT_TYPES), then debit_or_credit with statement convention ('credit' -> money in), then amount sign. The docstring clearly records the iteration-251 mis-step and the rationale for the revert — excellent for future maintainers.

New /{item_id}/diagnostic endpoint (lines 321-350) accepts EITHER UUID id OR txn_code via $or match — convenient for triage. Tenant-scoped, admin-gated via _require_admin, no _id leakage (projection {'_id': 0}), returns stored direction AND re-classified-now value so prod can spot drift. Good shape.

Endpoint placement matters: /{item_id}/diagnostic is declared BEFORE the catch-all /{txn_id}/tags PATCH etc., and AFTER /reclassify-direction and /sync/status/{job_id}, so static routes still win — verified by 404 test passing for unknown ids and the regression /sync/status/{unknown} test returning 404 (not falling through to diagnostic).
MINOR: raw_amount_sign uses 'positive' if (raw.amount or 0) > 0 — treats zero amount as 'zero' (correct), negative as 'negative'. OK.

MINOR (carried from prior): file is now 900 lines — still well past the 700-line guideline. Consider splitting into sync/list/tagging/proofs/diagnostic sub-modules in a future refactor (non-blocking).
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration_251_direction_classifier.py (2 unit-test assertions inverted + 1 seed payload updated to reflect the revert; same overall test count)

success_rate.backend: 100% (66/66 tests passing — 21 new iter-252 + 13 iter-251 updated + 12 iter-250 + 14 iter-249 + 8 iter-248)

seed_data_creation: Per-class fixtures seed/cleanup. iter-252 seeds DIAG-TEST-1 (id) + TXN-DIAG-1 (txn_code) into accounting_transactions for the diagnostic tests, deleted at fixture teardown. iter-251 seeds TEST-RECLASSIFY-* docs (also self-cleaning). No persistent test data left in DB.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 252 reverts the iter-251 classifier priority (transaction_type FIRST again) AND adds a new admin GET /api/accounting/transactions/{item_id}/diagnostic endpoint (accepts UUID id OR txn_code). The reclassify-direction endpoint is UNCHANGED. Two iter-251 unit-test assertions had to be inverted (vendor_payment+credit→debit, customer_payment+debit→credit) and one seed payload in the reclassify fixture was updated to ensure the flip still occurs under the reverted classifier. All other iter-248/249/250/251 regression tests pass unchanged. When testing future iterations, use pymongo (sync) for direct DB seed/read in tests — NOT motor — to avoid event-loop binding issues.
rca_of_the_issue: Prod reported many correctly-classified incoming UPI/NEFT credits (customer payments from Aparna Infrahousing, 17 Degree North Club, etc.) suddenly shown as money-OUT after iter-251. RCA: iter-251 had reordered _direction_of to check Zoho's debit_or_credit field BEFORE transaction_type. For Zoho bank-feed lines, debit_or_credit follows ACCOUNTING (bank-ledger) convention: money IN → bank asset debited → 'debit'; money OUT → asset credited → 'credit'. But the prior code interpreted it as STATEMENT convention (debit=money out). So all customer-payment credits were flipped wrongly. Fix: revert priority to transaction_type FIRST (curated _CREDIT_TYPES/_DEBIT_TYPES correctly classify the bulk of Zoho-categorised txns); fall back to debit_or_credit (statement convention) only when transaction_type isn't in either allowlist; amount sign as final fallback. Verified by unit test {transaction_type:customer_payment, debit_or_credit:debit} → 'credit' (was wrongly 'debit' under iter-251). The new diagnostic endpoint exposes the raw Zoho payload + the classifier's current verdict for any one row, so future edge cases can be triaged without blind iteration.
